---
title: "Outreach"
subtitle: "Mentoring, workshops, public talks, and AI literacy"
description: "BioMIND outreach across student mentoring, workshops, public talks, STEM education, AI literacy, and responsible AI education."
---

BioMIND is committed to building educational and outreach activities around **BioAI**, **AI4Science**, **trustworthy AI**, **human-centered AI**, and **complex systems**. Outreach activities may include student workshops, invited talks, public-facing AI literacy events, mentoring programs, and interdisciplinary research training.

## Areas of engagement

::: {.bm-grid .bm-grid-3}

::: {.bm-card}
[🎓]{.bm-card-icon}

### Student mentoring

Mentoring of undergraduate, master's, and Ph.D. students across the cluster's labs, including independent study, REU placements, and senior design projects.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
[🛠️]{.bm-card-icon}

### Workshops and tutorials

Hands-on workshops on BioAI, molecular ML, trustworthy AI, and complex-systems methods for students and partner organizations.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
[🗣️]{.bm-card-icon}

### Public talks

Lectures, panels, and invited talks for academic and public audiences on AI, biology, and intelligent systems.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
[🔬]{.bm-card-icon}

### STEM outreach

K–12 and community-college programs that introduce students to AI, computation, and the biological sciences.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
[📚]{.bm-card-icon}

### AI literacy

Educational content for non-specialists on what AI is, what it can and cannot do, and how to think critically about AI in society.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
[⚖️]{.bm-card-icon}

### Responsible AI education

Training and curriculum materials on responsible, fair, and human-centered AI for students, practitioners, and the public.
:::

::: {.bm-card}
[🧬]{.bm-card-icon}

### BioAI education

Programs that bridge biology and AI for students and researchers entering the field.
:::

:::

## Current initiatives

::: {.bm-callout .bm-callout-gold}
**Outreach activities are being compiled.** As cluster outreach activities are confirmed, they will be listed here with dates, audiences, and lead organizers.
:::

| Initiative | Audience | Lead | Status |
|------------|----------|------|--------|
| *Workshop / talk / program name* | Students / public / K–12 | TBD | Planned |
| *Workshop / talk / program name* | Students / public / K–12 | TBD | Planned |

## Get involved

- **Educators** — to bring a BioMIND speaker to your classroom or program, please use the [Contact](../contact.qmd) page.
- **Students** — see the [Faculty](../people/faculty.qmd) and [Research](../research/index.qmd) pages for mentor and project information.
- **Community partners** — we welcome conversations about workshops, public events, and educational collaboration.
